title: Source-qualified kind keys (kind/agda/module)
body: Early registry used keys like 'kind/agda/module' that baked source-provenance into the kind name. This is hardcoded taxonomy — kind and source should be independent K's whose correspondence emerges via wedges. Replaced by separate kind/ and source/ families, then by the flat pool directive.

title: Per-family weight scaling in calibration
body: Calibration tuned per-family scalars (token_scale, name_tok_scale, kind_scale, ...). This presupposes the family taxonomy and scales entire families as blocks. Under the flat-K-pool principle, families don't exist at registration; there is nothing to scale family-wise. Weight adjustment is per-K or via a non-family-aware gradient.

title: Lowercase canonicalization of kind strings
body: Attempted to lowercase deep_kind_set() output so Agda 'function' and Python 'FunctionDef' (case-collapsed to 'functiondef') might share a bit. User explicitly rejected: 'that's still a hardcoded mapping at a higher level'. Any cross-source equivalence must emerge from residue-driven wedge discovery; lowercasing is an asserted equivalence the pipeline should not make.

title: Registering 'arity/N' as a primitive K
body: Proposed to extract arity (number of parameters) from decls and register 'arity/4' etc. as K's to enable cross-source matching of same-arity functions. User rejected: 'arity tracking should be emergent and composed; foreknowledge of that is cheating'. Arity is the grade of a discovered wedge, not a feature the extractor computes and registers.

title: Hand-written pattern matchers for specific axioms
body: Briefly considered writing subtree-pattern matchers for axioms like 'eval(d1+d2,a) = eval(d1,a)+eval(d2,a)'. User stopped it immediately: 'and that should be emergent, not bespoke'. Axiom recognition is a high-grade wedge the loop should earn; writing a dedicated recognizer anywhere breaks self-hosting.

title: Commit predicate = pair earns Boolean on some K-pair
body: Interpretation (b) from a mid-session clarification: a thing-pair (A, B) is 'committed/aligned' iff there exists (K_i, K_j) in pool such that (K_i fires on A, K_j gaps on A) AND (K_j fires on B, K_i gaps on B). User corrected: this is still a commit gate, which throws away the pre-Boolean evidence the framework feeds on. Alignment is a distribution; Boolean-earning is one reading of that distribution, not a gate.

title: max_iters parameter in calibration loop
body: The prior calibrate_weights.py had max_iters=8-10 and stopped after that many iterations. This is external scheduling that violates closure. Termination must be self-determined by the state evolution, not a counter.

title: triples.jsonl with (agda, paper, python) columns
body: Prior pipeline's primary output was triples.jsonl with three named columns. Bakes in the three-source taxonomy AND the 3-valency of the grouping, both of which should be emergent. N-valent correspondences at various grades emerge in the evidence data; the output format should enumerate them without pre-assigning column roles.
